events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked: "emergency fixture on switched leg". This is an electrical installation. The emergency fixture (likely an emergency light) is on a switched leg, meaning it is controlled by a switch. This is likely a violation because emergency lighting should be on an unswitched circuit or have a means to ensure it's always on. NEC 700.12(F) maybe? Actually, NEC 700.12(F) requires that branch circuits supplying emergency lighting be arranged so that they are not subject to the same switch as normal lighting. But "switched leg" might mean that the emergency fixture is on a circuit that is switched, which could turn off the emergency light inadvertently. This is a non-compliant installation. The work stage is "rough-complete" (roughed in). So decision: fail. Risk: could be high or critical because if the switch is turned off, emergency lighting would be off, compromising egress. I'd say critical. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: maybe "non-compliant" and "installed-defective"? Defective? It's installed incorrectly, so "non-compliant" fits. "installed-defective" might be if the component itself is defective, but here it's the wiring method. So just "non-compliant". Percent_complete: maybe 80%? Rough complete, but not final. Value: 0? Not applicable. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time: 0. Rate: 0. Order: none. Workable: none. Findings: ["Emergency fixture connected to switched leg, could be turned off with normal lighting, compromising egress."]. Actions: ["Re-wire emergency fixture to an unswitched circuit or install a bypass switch that ensures automatic operation upon loss of normal power."]. Rationale: "NEC 700.12(F) requires emergency lighting circuits to be arranged so that they will not be affected by the switching of normal lighting. Connecting an emergency fixture to a switched leg risks it being turned off when the switch is off, which is non-compliant and dangerous." References: ["NEC 700.12(F)", "NFPA 101"]. I'll output that JSON.
